summary: Tier 1: strip {p. N} braces + 'Buy this Book' preamble + plate/figure + re-chunk/re-embed
description:
Tier 1 (cont.): strip scan/front-matter artifacts that remain after C1's nav strip.

Patterns:
- \{\s*p\.\s*\d+\s*\}   — brace page markers (121 local chunks). SAFE to strip.
  Do NOT strip bare "p. N" (no braces) — caveat #2: it can be a legitimate citation.
- "Buy this Book at Amazon.com <Title>, <Translator>, tr. [<year>], at sacred-texts.com"
  — sacred-texts book-front-matter preamble on first pages (21 chunks; surfaced by
  todo:8abbb645 once the nav prefix was stripped — e.g. bundahishn chunk 001 now
  starts "Buy this Book at Amazon.com Pahlavi Texts, Part I (SBE05) , E.W. West,
  tr. [1880], at sacred-texts.com p. ix ..."). Candidate pattern (validate like C1):
  Buy this Book at Amazon\.com\b.*?\bat sacred-texts\.com\s*  (non-greedy, bounded
  by the closing "at sacred-texts.com"; leaves the following bare "p. ix" citation).
- Plate/figure/scan apparatus: "click to enlarge", \bPlate\s+[IVXLC\d], \bFig\.
  (~110 in live v27) — image captions from the scans.

Mechanism: the brace-marker and "Buy this Book" patterns are uniform sacred-texts
artifacts (like the nav), so add them to scripts/chunk.py BASELINE_PRE_STRIP
alongside the C1 nav pattern — one place, covers all sources, no per-config edits.
Plate/figure is narrower; use per-config pre_strip_patterns if it's text-specific.
Re-chunk + re-embed affected (same refresh sequence as C1: clear dir → chunk.py
--only → bootstrap_chunks → cleanup_stale → embed_corpus). Validate 0 false-positives
across the corpus before applying, exactly as C1 did.

Done contract:
- 0 chunk bodies contain the {p. N} brace form or the "Buy this Book ... at
  sacred-texts.com" preamble; plate/figure caption fragments gone.
- Bare "p. N" citations preserved (spot-check); 0 false-positives corpus-wide.
- Round-trip green; embeddings refreshed.
- Regression test: extend test_no_sacred_texts_nav_prefix (or a sibling) to assert
  no chunk body contains \{\s*p\.\s*\d+\s*\} or "Buy this Book at Amazon.com".
